Off Calendar Jul-27-2022 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-02-2021 Set For Case Management Conference On Aug-17-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion To Stay Pending Arbitration And Motion To Stay Pending Arbitration; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Oct-29-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. SARA EHSANI-NIA, Esq. of Bibiyan Law Group, P.C. (Tel: 310-438-5555) for plaintiff James Caramazza;
Atty. BRIAN S. FONG, Esq. of Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton LLP (Tel: 415-434-9100) for defendants Greystar Management Services, LP and Sunrise Riverside, LLC.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS GREYSTAR MANAGEMENT SERVICES, LP, AND SUNRISE RIVERSIDE, LLC's Motion To Stay Pending Arbitration And Motion To Stay Pending Arbitration.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' motion to stay this private attorney general case is granted. Claims alleging largely the same conduct by the same defendants under the same wage and hour laws were ordered to arbitration a year ago.

Plaintiff filed a putative class action on February 21, 2020. Plaintiff's individual claims were ordered to arbitration on October 7, 2020 and the class action was stayed. Plaintiff filed this PAGA action with largely the same wage and hour allegations on August 4, 2020. Both sides report extensive settlement negotiations.

A stay pending resolution of the arbitration will be efficient for both the courts and the parties. Whether the arbitration has preclusive effect or not, its results will no doubt inform the settlement efforts - which is how a large majority of wage and hour cases are resolved.

Plaintiff claims a "wealth of authority that states that a stay under these circumstances is not proper," but cites none. In Kim v. Reins International California, Inc. (2020) 9 Cal.5th 73, "[t]he PAGA litigation was stayed until arbitration was complete," a procedure not found improper. (Id. at 82.) Jarboe v. Hanlees Auto Group (2020) 53 Cal.App.5th 539 dealt with claims severance within one case, which is not our circumstance. In any event, as Jarboe states, "the trial court has the discretion to stay proceedings on the inarbitral claims pending resolution of the arbitration." (Id. at 556.)

Counsel for defendants to prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/RBU)
